items.briefing:
Deepfake fraud attacks have surged 180%, meaning scammers are increasingly using AI-generated fake identities to bypass identity verification — a real risk for RTOs handling enrolments and student ID checks online. Separately, cybercriminals are now hiding invisible text inside emails to trick AI-based spam filters, meaning malicious emails may slip through security tools your organisation relies on.

Across the region, experts are warning that AI security tools still need strong human oversight — don't assume automated systems will catch everything. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unusual request to verify someone's identity or approve a payment, always confirm through a second channel like a phone call.

featured.title: DigiCert survey finds 81% of Australian organisations hit by AI security incidents or vulnerabilities
featured.quote: 81% of Australian organisations already experiencing AI security incidents reveals urgent industry-wide vulnerability.
